sourceMovie.title: Blue Is the Warmest Color
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Blue Is the Warmest Color (2013) as a romance theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The romance and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Call Me by Your Name
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Blue Is the Warmest Color clicked for you, Call Me by Your Name (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Blue Is the Warmest Color?
faq.answer: Try Call Me by Your Name, CODA, Chasing Amy—each shares DNA with Blue Is the Warmest Color while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Blue Is the Warmest Color?
faq.answer: Blue Is the Warmest Color (2013) is best described as Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Blue Is the Warmest Color worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
